title: Plasma Insulin Measurement by ELISA
author: Henri Brunengraber
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yesftee

steps.title: Protocol
steps.instruction: Protocol: 1. Plasma or serum are isolated from mice fasted or after glucose injection. Heparin or EDTA coated tubes are acceptable for plasma fractionation. 2. Insulin is measured according to the manufacturer’s directions.

steps.title: Manufacturer's Protocol below
steps.instruction: Manufacturer's Protocol below: MOUSE INSULIN ELISA KIT 96-Well Plate (Cat. #10-1247-01)

steps.title: capture of insulin molecules from samples...
steps.instruction: capture of insulin molecules from samples to the wells of a microtiter plate coated by pre-titered amount of a monoclonal mouse anti-rat insulin antibodies and the binding of biotinylated polyclonal antibodies to the captured insulin,

steps.title: wash away of unbound materials from samples,
steps.instruction: wash away of unbound materials from samples,

steps.title: binding of horseradish peroxidase to the immobilized biotinylated antibodies,
steps.instruction: binding of horseradish peroxidase to the immobilized biotinylated antibodies,

steps.title: wash away of free enzyme conjugates, and
steps.instruction: wash away of free enzyme conjugates, and

steps.title: quantification of immobilized antibody- enzyme conjugates...
steps.instruction: quantification of immobilized antibody- enzyme conjugates by monitoring horseradish peroxidase activities in the presence of the substrate 3,3',5,5'-tetramethylbenzidine. The enzyme activity is measured spectrophotometrically by the increased absorbency at 450 nm, corrected from the absorbency at 590nm, after acidification of formed products. Since the increase in absorbency is directly proportional to the amount of captured insulin in the unknown sample, the latter can be derived by interpolation from a reference curve generated in the same assay with reference standards of known concentrations of rat insulin.
